title: pomaderris paniculosa subsp. novae-zelandiae
intro: Pomaderris paniculosa subsp. novae-zelandiae, a subspecies of the Pomaderris paniculosa plant, is a native New Zealand shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Rhamnaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The leaves and stems of Pomaderris paniculosa subsp. novae-zelandiae have been found to have numerous health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and stems are prepared as infusions or decoctions, and can also be dried and used in herbal teas or as a topical remedy.
